question.text: A QRTA's wind-down plan is required to ensure:

answer.text: Orderly transition of services to another RTA if the QRTA ceases operations
Quick closure of all investor accounts within 30 days
Transfer of all assets to SEBI's custody
Automatic merger with the other QRTA in the industry

explanation.text: **The Logic:** QRTAs must maintain a wind-down plan that outlines how services will be orderly transitioned to another RTA if the QRTA ceases operations. This ensures investor service continuity and data preservation during any transition. **The Trap:** Wind-down doesn't mean shutting down and closing accounts. It means systematic transition planning to prevent service disruption to millions of investors. **Cross-Exam Context:** Wind-down planning is common for systemically important financial institutions. The concept parallels bank resolution planning and insurance company wind-down frameworks. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The existence of wind-down plans provides comfort that even in an extreme scenario of an RTA ceasing operations, your clients' records and services will be preserved and transferred. This systemic safeguard is built into the framework.
